evals.name: bug-regression-stj
evals.expected_output: Bug report with confirmed regression from .NET 8 to .NET 9+ in System.Text.Json. Should recommend KEEP with high priority. Should identify as regression and note the STJ area label is correct.

assertions.name: correct-classification
assertions.description: Issue is classified as Bug report

assertions.name: regression-detected
assertions.description: Report identifies this as a regression

assertions.name: keep-recommendation
assertions.description: Recommendation is KEEP (this is a valid, reproducible regression)

assertions.name: area-label-correct
assertions.description: Recognizes area-System.Text.Json is the correct label

evals.name: api-proposal-marshal
evals.prompt: Can you triage this issue for me? #125197
evals.expected_output: API proposal for MarshalContentsAttribute. Should classify as API proposal, assess merit, check for prior art in the marshalling/interop space, and provide a KEEP or NEEDS INFO recommendation.

assertions.name: correct-classification
assertions.description: Issue is classified as API Proposal

assertions.name: has-prior-art
assertions.description: Report includes prior art / ecosystem research

assertions.name: has-complexity-estimate
assertions.description: Report includes a complexity estimate

evals.name: question-reveals-bug
evals.expected_output: Labeled as 'question' but actually describes a regression (AssemblyName accepted invalid names in .NET 10 but not .NET 8). The skill should recognize this is actually a bug, not a question, and flag the mislabeling.

assertions.name: mislabel-detected
assertions.description: Report identifies that the 'question' label is wrong -- this is actually a bug

assertions.name: regression-noted
assertions.description: Notes that this is a regression from .NET 8

evals.name: question-dotnet-framework
evals.prompt: What do you think about this issue? https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/123303
evals.expected_output: This is a .NET Framework question about Windows Services on ARM64. Should identify it as out of scope for dotnet/runtime (which tracks modern .NET), and recommend CLOSE or redirect to Developer Community.

assertions.name: framework-identified
assertions.description: Report identifies this is a .NET Framework issue, not modern .NET

assertions.name: close-recommendation
assertions.description: Recommends CLOSE since this is .NET Framework

evals.name: enhancement-cdac
evals.expected_output: Enhancement request for cDac IRuntimeTypeSystem APIs. Should classify as enhancement, assess feasibility, and provide a KEEP recommendation since it's from a team member addressing known limitations.

assertions.name: correct-classification
assertions.description: Issue is classified as Enhancement

assertions.name: has-assessment
assertions.description: Report includes an assessment section

evals.name: question-framework-path
evals.expected_output: Question about Path.GetInvalidPathChars in .NET Framework 4.7.2. Should identify this as a .NET Framework question and recommend CLOSE with redirect to Developer Community. Should also provide an answer explaining the behavior.

assertions.name: framework-identified
assertions.description: Report identifies this as .NET Framework, not modern .NET

assertions.name: close-recommendation
assertions.description: Recommends CLOSE

evals.name: perf-regression-autofiler
evals.expected_output: Automated performance regression filed by performanceautofiler bot. Should classify as performance regression, identify the component and severity, and provide a KEEP recommendation with the regression data analyzed.

assertions.name: correct-classification
assertions.description: Issue is classified as Performance regression

assertions.name: has-label-check
assertions.description: Report includes a label check section

evals.name: mono-question-gc-safety
evals.prompt: Can you evaluate issue 122969 for me?
evals.expected_output: Detailed Mono interop/GC safety question. Should classify as question, note it's Mono-specific, provide a helpful answer about GC handle requirements, and recommend CLOSE (convert to Discussion) or answer inline.

assertions.name: correct-classification
assertions.description: Issue is classified as Question

assertions.name: mono-identified
assertions.description: Report identifies this as Mono-specific

assertions.name: has-answer
assertions.description: Report includes an answer to the question
